terms.definition: A term, semester, or grading period. OneRoster models these explicitly as `academicSessions`.
Assignment & Grade Services 2.0 — the LTI Advantage service that syncs grades from a tool into the LMS gradebook.
A 1EdTech specification with a structured vocabulary for capturing learning/usage events (Actor–Action–Object) in JSON-LD; current version 1.2.
A time-based measure of academic study introduced in 1906; the historical basis of the higher-ed credit hour.
Common Education Data Standards — a national common vocabulary/data dictionary of shared education data elements, maintained by NCES (U.S. Dept of Education).
Total estimated annual cost to attend (tuition, housing, books, etc.); the starting point for need.
An education model where progress is based on demonstrated mastery of competencies rather than time/seat hours.
US federal unit of student work (34 CFR § 600.2): ≈ 1 hour of instruction + 2 hours of out-of-class work weekly for ~15 weeks.
LTI Advantage service for selecting tool content from within the LMS and embedding it.
A progress report mapping a student's transcript against degree/major/minor requirements in real time.
Measuring learning by directly assessing knowledge/skills/abilities in lieu of credit or clock hours (34 CFR § 668.10).
FERPA category of non-harmful info (e.g., name, dates of attendance) that may be disclosed without consent unless the student opts out; excludes SSN.

An open-source education Data Standard + Unifying Data Model plus the ODS/API technology suite, backed by the Michael & Susan Dell Foundation, used to connect SIS, assessment, rostering, and LMS data.
Electronic Data Interchange — machine-to-machine structured-data exchange; PESC's transcript EDI standards date to 1990.
A record directly related to a student and maintained by an educational institution or party acting for it.
A student who is 18+ or enrolled in a postsecondary institution; FERPA rights transfer from parent to this student.
Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act — US federal law (20 U.S.C. § 1232g; 34 CFR Part 99) giving students/parents rights over education records and limiting disclosure of PII.
The credit hours counted toward GPA (the GPA denominator); not all enrolled credits always count.
The registrar's final audit after final grades confirming all requirements are met.
A signed JWT issued by an OpenID Provider asserting the end-user's identity in OIDC.
The party that authenticates the user and issues assertions/tokens (e.g., a campus SSO).
Integrated Postsecondary Education Data System — NCES's mandatory annual surveys for Title IV institutions.
Institutional Student Information Record — the processed FAFSA data (including federal tax info) sent to schools.
A PESC workgroup effort building a JSON-LD version of the Transcript standard alongside the XML one.
Learning Management System (e.g., Canvas, Moodle) — delivers course content and activities; it consumes rosters from and returns grades to the SIS.
Learning Tools Interoperability (1EdTech) — standard for launching external tools/content from an LMS; v1.3 uses OIDC + signed JWTs.
A Caliper domain vocabulary that constrains which actions/events are valid (e.g., assessment, media).
National Center for Education Statistics, within the U.S. Department of Education; operates IPEDS.
Names & Role Provisioning Services 2.0 — LTI Advantage service that shares a course's roster/enrollments with a tool.
Ed-Fi's Operational Data Store and its REST API — the technology suite that stores and moves data shaped by the Ed-Fi Data Standard.

A 1EdTech standard for securely exchanging roster info, course materials, and grades between systems (e.g., SIS ↔ LMS), via CSV bulk or REST. v1.2 splits into Rostering, Gradebook, and Resource services.
Postsecondary Electronic Standards Council — develops free, open education data standards (e.g., the College Transcript).
grade points × credit hours for a course; summed across courses for the GPA numerator.
Return of Title IV Funds — recalculation of earned aid when a recipient withdraws; past 60% of the term, all aid is earned.

sources.title: Return of Title IV Funds — FSA

terms.definition: One of the three IPEDS collection windows: Fall, Winter, or Spring.
A grouped set of degree/major/minor rules (credits, GPA, specific courses) the audit evaluates.
Provisioning students, teachers, and classes into systems. OneRoster's Rostering service standardizes this exchange.
Index from the FAFSA used to determine federal aid eligibility (replaced the EFC in 2024-25); can be negative, as low as −1,500.
A signed XML packet of security statements (authentication, attribute, authorization decision) about a subject.
The set of course sections offered in a term. The prior like-term's schedule is the template used during term rollover.

sources.title: Class Scheduling Workflow — Penn State Registrar

terms.definition: Credit earned based on time enrolled/instructional hours — the assumption CBE departs from.
The Caliper library embedded in a tool that emits learning-event data.
Student Information System — the system of record for students, enrollment, courses, grades, and academic records. ManageArk is an enterprise-scale, cloud-native SIS.
Service Provider (SAML) / Relying Party (OIDC) — the application that relies on the IdP to authenticate users.
A server for secure electronic exchange of student records (EDI), operated by the National Student Clearinghouse.
Curriculum built around predetermined state/national learning standards, with assessments aligned to those standards. Note: “SBC” is not a single standardized acronym in education.
A grading approach that reports a student's mastery of specific learning standards rather than averaging unrelated points into one score.
One IPEDS data collection (e.g., Completions, Finance, Fall Enrollment); there are 13 across three periods.
The SIS process of setting up a new term by copying the prior like-term's schedule of classes forward as a template; instructor assignments, notes, and cancelled sections typically do not carry over.
US federal student-aid programs (e.g., Pell, federal loans); CBE programs need approval to be Title IV-eligible.
A non-destructive simulation applying a hypothetical major/minor to existing courses.
Experience API by ADL — an open, extensible JSON statement format for learning records; an alternative to Caliper.
